labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after initiation or dose increase, then within 2-4 weeks after each dose change, then annually
labs.initialMonitoring: within 2-4 weeks of initiation or increase in dose
labs.actionRequired: Continue if <30% increase; investigate and consider dose reduction or discontinuation if >30% increase within 4 weeks
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for acute kidney injury and assess tolerability of RAS blockade

labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after initiation or dose increase, then within 2-4 weeks after each dose change, then annually
labs.initialMonitoring: within 2-4 weeks of initiation or increase in dose
labs.actionRequired: Manage hyperkalemia with dietary restriction, diuretics, sodium bicarbonate, or potassium binders; reduce dose or stop if uncontrolled
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for hyperkalemia, especially in patients with eGFR <30 ml/min per 1.73 m2

labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after initiation or dose increase, then ongoing monitoring
labs.initialMonitoring: within 2-4 weeks of initiation or increase in dose
labs.actionRequired: Discontinue other concurrent blood pressure medications before reducing ARB dose; reduce dose or discontinue if symptomatic hypotension persists

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: Close monitoring of serum potassium required when eGFR <30 ml/min per 1.73 m2. Discontinue in pregnancy. Reduce dose or discontinue when eGFR <15 ml/min per 1.73 m2 to reduce uremic symptoms.
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: Monitor for changes in blood pressure, serum creatinine, and serum potassium within 2–4 weeks of initiation or increase in the dose of an ACEi or ARB. Continue ACEi or ARB therapy unless serum creatinine rises by more than 30% within 4 weeks following initiation of treatment or an increase in dose.

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease
